title: PMA (Parts Manufacturer Approval) Landscape: Actionable Arbitrage Intelligence

key_growth_drivers: OEM supply chain strain creating urgency for alternative parts
Aircraft delivery delays forcing airlines to keep older fleets longer
Advances in manufacturing (3D printing, new materials)
Growing cooperation between OEM type certificate holders and PMA companies

discount_vs_oem.discount_over_time: Grows to 50-60% over 5 years because HEICO does not pass through OEM-level price increases
discount_vs_oem.typical_range: 20-60% depending on part category and time since PMA entry

aircraft_engine_coverage.aircraft_types: Large commercial, regional, business, and military aircraft
aircraft_engine_coverage.engine_platforms: Nearly every engine platform (CFM56, LEAP, CF6, GE90, V2500, PW4000, etc.)

wencor_acquisition.price: $1.9 billion cash + $150 million in HEICO Class A stock
wencor_acquisition.strategic_value: Largest HEICO acquisition ever; little to no PMA overlap
wencor_capabilities: CMM and DER component maintenance repair

heico_corporation.pma_management_program: Comprehensive solution including HEICO parts, third-party PMA sourcing, quality audits, and tailored cost-saving strategies
heico_corporation.competitive_moat: Scale advantage is enormous: 19,500+ parts vs <2,000 for nearest competitor. Each new PMA costs $50K-$500K+ to certify, creating massive barrier to entry.

chromalloy.description: Only independent provider of FAA-approved PMA parts and DER repairs for hot section of aerospace engines
chromalloy.safety_record: 6 billion+ flight hours, ZERO airworthiness directives

cf6_80c2.parts: HPT Stage 1 and Stage 2 turbine blades
cf6_80c2.anniversary: 20th anniversary of 1st stage HPT blade PMA celebrated
cf6_80c2.distribution: AAR Corp exclusive distributor for global aftermarket (Nov 2024)

cfm56_5b_7b.hpt_blade: FAA approved Oct 2025; backlog sold out through 2025, customers reserving 2026 slots
cfm56_5b_7b.lpt_stage1_ngv: FAA approved; includes 'up-change' part number covering updated configuration
cfm56_5b_7b.total_planned_parts: 5 hot-section parts planned for FTAI joint venture

v2500_selectone.parts_in_development: 6 different turbine blade types with Lufthansa Technik

cost_savings.cfm56_hpt_vane_example: PMA sells for ~$5,000 vs GE OEM at ~$20,000 (75% discount)
cost_savings.cf6_50_overhaul_reduction: 20-25% reduction in total engine overhaul cost through PMA/DER

ftai_joint_venture.ftai_royalty_to_chromalloy: 25% of sourced profit to third parties

chromalloy.market_bifurcation_thesis: Operators of mid-life engines (CFM56, V2500) focus on cost-of-ownership over residual value, making PMA highly attractive. Younger engines (LEAP) still weight residual value, so OEM parts preferred.

jet_parts_engineering.specialty: Reverse-engineered FAA-PMA parts and DER repairs for expendable aircraft components
jet_parts_engineering.market_commentary: PMA market remains strong with steady expansion from long-time users and adoption from first-time customers

pma_aeroparts.value_prop: Shorter lead times and substantial cost savings vs OEM

spirit_aerosystems.status: Major PMA market player (now part of Boeing)

delta_flight_products.recent_pma: Received FAA PMA from MIDO for certain replacement aircraft parts
delta_flight_products.significance: Airlines themselves are becoming PMA manufacturers

honeywell_aerospace.note: In Jan 2024, received FAA approval for PMA parts for CFM56-5B engine
honeywell_aerospace.significance: Even large OEMs are pursuing PMA for competitor engines

transdigm_business_model.strategy: Acquire companies with sole-source contracts, raise prices

why_pma_struggles_against_transdigm: PMA only economical for parts retailing $5,000+; 90% of TransDigm parts below this threshold
Low-value, low-volume parts are extremely difficult/uneconomical to PMA
Up-front design and qualification costs too high relative to addressable revenue on small parts
TransDigm's proprietary designs include unique IP and features harder to replicate
Airlines find it hard to switch away from sole-source parts embedded in their maintenance programs
where_pma_can_win: Parts where TransDigm has raised prices aggressively, creating economic incentive for PMA
Categories where multiple airlines request alternatives simultaneously

pentagon_dod_audit_findings.excess_profit_identified: $20.8 million on 105 spare parts over 2.5 years
pentagon_dod_audit_findings.case_study_metal_pin: $4,361 charged for half-inch metal pin (9,400% profit)
pentagon_dod_audit_findings.case_study_clutch_disk: $1,443 charged for part costing $32 to make
pentagon_dod_audit_findings.data_disclosure: TransDigm refused cost/pricing data for 15 of 16 sampled contracts (2015-2017)
pentagon_dod_audit_findings.regulatory_loophole: 95%+ of DOD contracts fell below mandatory disclosure thresholds
pentagon_dod_audit_findings.congressional_response: Bipartisan outrage; lawmakers pushing anti-gouging legislation for military parts

transdigm_vs_pma_battle.arbitrage_implication: TransDigm parts >$5,000 with military AND commercial demand are the most attractive PMA targets. DOD audits publicly identify the most overpriced parts, creating a roadmap for PMA opportunity.

faa_pma_approval_data.database: FAA Dynamic Regulatory System (DRS) at drs.faa.gov
faa_pma_approval_data.note: Granular 2024-2025 approval counts not publicly aggregated; must be queried from DRS database directly

airline_pma_adoption.overall_trend: Adoption accelerating, driven by supply chain strain and cost pressure
airline_pma_adoption.primary_driver_2024_2025: Parts availability (not just cost) - 'need for parts NOW' is dominant factor

delta_air_lines.status: One of the strongest PMA proponents in the industry
delta_air_lines.activity: Delta TechOps has developed and certified hundreds of PMA parts in-house
delta_air_lines.unique: Delta Flight Products now holds its own PMA manufacturing approval

american_airlines.status: Active in MARPA air carrier committee
american_airlines.involvement: Engaged in PMA adoption discussions at industry level

united_airlines.status: Active in MARPA air carrier committee
united_airlines.involvement: Engaged in PMA adoption discussions at industry level

british_airways.note: HEICO received BA's first PMA parts order (historic milestone for European acceptance)

lufthansa_group.status: Deep PMA engagement through Lufthansa Technik
lufthansa_group.partnerships: Joint PMA development with Chromalloy (V2500, CF6, CFM56)
lufthansa_group.ownership: Lufthansa Technik holds 20% stake in HEICO Aerospace

low_cost_carriers.trend: Growth of regional and low-cost carriers increases PMA demand
low_cost_carriers.driver: Cost optimization imperative makes PMA highly attractive

airline_pma_adoption.leasing_company_acceptance: Mixed - lessors focused on residual value may resist PMA for younger engines, but mid-life assets increasingly accept PMA

opportunity_1_cfm56_hot_section.description: CFM56 engine PMA parts are the hottest segment in PMA right now
opportunity_1_cfm56_hot_section.why: CFM56 has largest installed base, engines are mid-life, operators focused on cost not residual value
opportunity_1_cfm56_hot_section.savings: $2M per shop visit with full PMA suite; individual parts 60-75% below OEM
opportunity_1_cfm56_hot_section.demand_signal: Chromalloy backlog sold out through end of 2025, customers booking 2026
opportunity_1_cfm56_hot_section.action: Source CFM56 PMA parts from Chromalloy/AAR channel; sell to independent MROs not yet using PMA

opportunity_2_transdigm_high_value_parts.description: Identify TransDigm sole-source parts >$5,000 with PMA alternatives
opportunity_2_transdigm_high_value_parts.why: DOD audits reveal specific parts with 1,000-9,000%+ margins; PMA alternatives dramatically undercut
opportunity_2_transdigm_high_value_parts.action: Cross-reference TransDigm subsidiary part numbers against HEICO PMA catalog and FAA DRS for alternatives
opportunity_2_transdigm_high_value_parts.data_source: DOD IG reports publicly name overpriced parts; FAA DRS shows PMA alternatives

opportunity_3_v2500_pma_emerging.description: V2500 engine PMA is earlier-stage than CFM56 - get in early
opportunity_3_v2500_pma_emerging.why: Chromalloy + Lufthansa Technik developing 6 blade types; only 2 certified so far
opportunity_3_v2500_pma_emerging.action: Monitor Chromalloy/LHT announcements for new V2500 PMA certifications; position as early supplier to V2500 MRO shops

opportunity_4_pma_for_new_gen_engines.description: LEAP and GTF engines will need PMA in 3-5 years as they enter mid-life
opportunity_4_pma_for_new_gen_engines.why: Current PMA focus is legacy engines; first-mover advantage on LEAP/GTF PMA distribution
opportunity_4_pma_for_new_gen_engines.timeline: LEAP fleet entering first shop visits now; PMA development takes 2-3 years
opportunity_4_pma_for_new_gen_engines.action: Track HEICO and others' LEAP PMA filings in FAA DRS; build relationships with MROs servicing LEAP

opportunity_5_asia_pacific_distribution.description: APAC is fastest-growing PMA market (30%+ share, 41% of growth)
opportunity_5_asia_pacific_distribution.why: Asian carriers rapidly expanding fleets, cost-sensitive, increasingly accepting PMA
opportunity_5_asia_pacific_distribution.action: Partner with HEICO/Chromalloy for APAC distribution of PMA parts

key_data_sources_for_monitoring.what_to_monitor: New PMA approvals by company, engine type, and part category
New PMA counts, FSG revenue growth, discount trends, new aircraft/engine coverage
New engine PMA certifications, FTAI JV progress, V2500 approvals
Industry adoption trends, new members, regulatory changes
TransDigm audit findings identifying overpriced sole-source parts
Chromalloy PMA progress, module cost savings, new engine platforms
PMA acceptance trends, new entrants, OEM responses
